response:
The fare depends on how far the taxi travels beyond the initial four kilometers. Any distance over that limit adds two yuan for each extra kilometer rounded up. This rule creates a clear link between the distance variable and the money charged. The spring of the season brings many travelers to the airport every single day. Drivers must calculate their earnings based on these specific distance rules.

A passenger paying thirty eight yuan implies a total distance of twenty two kilometers was charged. Since the actual road distance is fifteen kilometers, the extra seven kilometers come from waiting time. Every five minutes of stopping equals one kilometer of added distance in this city. This means the taxi stopped for thirty five minutes at most during the trip. The spring weather often causes traffic delays that increase these waiting periods.
